description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the docker image with a docker:dind service, defining the stages ubuntu, rustup, rust, badger, and build, and a global before_script that installs curl and logs in to Docker with `docker login -u rustdocker -p $DOCKER_KEY`. Add image:ubuntu and image:rustup jobs in the ubuntu and rustup stages respectively, both limited to the master branch, each running `BUILD_DATE=$(date -u +"%Y%m%d")`, building their Docker images (`docker build --pull --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$BUILD_DATE -t $UBUNTU:$BUILD_DATE ubuntu-dev` or `docker build --pull --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$BUILD_DATE -t $RUSTUP:$BUILD_DATE rustup`), tagging them as latest, and pushing both tags with `docker push`. Add three rust jobs (image:rust:beta, image:rust:nightly, image:rust:stable) in the rust stage, all only on master, each setting RUST_VERSION to beta, nightly, or the $STABLE variable, then executing the shared script that sets BUILD_DATE, builds the rust image with `docker build --pull --build-arg CHANNEL=$RUST_VERSION --build-arg BUILD_DATE=$BUILD_DATE --build-arg RUST_VERSION=$RUST_VERSION -t $RUST:$RUST_VERSION rust`, tags the image as $RUSTFMT and $RUSTFMT_CLIPPY with the same version, and pushes all three images; the stable job also runs an after_script that retags the images with the literal tag "stable" and pushes those tags. Add a hidden .badger job in the badger stage that iterates over $WEBHOOKS and posts each webhook with `for webhook in $WEBHOOKS; do curl -X POST $webhook; done`. Add two hidden build jobs: .build:on-commit runs in the build stage for all pipelines except schedules and executes `pwd`, `ls -l`, `which sh`, and `sh ./refresh.sh`; .build:on-schedule runs only for scheduled pipelines in the build stage and executes `sh ./refresh.sh`.
